Vlog Social Media Challenge

Create a 2-member team vlog, post it on Instagram & win!

🚀 Phase 1 — Link Submission Active
📸 Instagram Only
👥 2 Members / Team

📋 Rules & Guidelines

  • Each team must have exactly 2 members
  • Maximum 2 teams per class — choose wisely!
  • Post your vlog as an Instagram Reel only
  • Your Instagram profile must be public
  • Must use the official challenge hashtag in caption
  • Vlog must be original — no recycled content
  • Minimum duration: 30 seconds
  • Each team picks a different topic (no repeats in class)
  • Submit your Instagram Reel link below by deadline

🎬 Vlog Topics (Choose One)

✈️
✈️ Travel Vlog
Explore a place — campus, market, landmark
🍽️
🍽️ Food Vlog
Explore food, restaurants, street eats
🎉
🎉 Event Vlog
Cover a college event, fest, or activity
🌅
🌅 Daily Life Vlog
A day in your life — routines, moments, lifestyle

🏆 Scoring System — How Winners Are Decided

50%
📊 Social Media Score
Auto-tracked via hashtag
Likes + Comments + Views
Combined engagement score
50%
👨‍⚖️ Jury Score
Evaluated by 2 judges
Content · Creativity · Presentation
Average of both jury marks
🏅 Final Score = (Social Score × 50%) + (Jury Score × 50%) — Top team per class advances to final evaluation

YouTube Advertisement Challenge

Create a class advertisement, upload to YouTube & win!

▶️ YouTube Only
🏫 Class Event — 1 Team Per Class
👥 8 Members
🏆 All Classes Eligible

📋 Rules & Guidelines

  • Each class forms 1 team of 8 members
  • Create an original advertisement video
  • Upload to YouTube with a public/unlisted link
  • Video must be original — no copied content
  • Any product, service, or social cause allowed
  • Submit your YouTube video link below by deadline
  • The same link will be used for scoring

🏆 How Winners Are Decided

50%
📊 YouTube Engagement
Views + Likes + Original Comments
50%
👨‍⚖️ Jury Score
Jury 1 + Jury 2 averaged (combined)
🏅 Final = (Social×50%) + (Jury×50%)

💳 Payment Approval — Admin Panel

Each class registers as a group and pays ₹1,500. Coordinators verify the payment in person, then approve the entire class here.

✅ How Payment Approval Works (Per Class)

1️⃣

All students of a class register — their class appears here as ⏳ Pending

2️⃣

Class representative pays ₹1,500 cash to Lalitha S K or Priyanka V on behalf of the class

3️⃣

Coordinator (Lalitha S K / Priyanka V) opens this website → selects Manager login → enters manager password → goes to "💳 Payment Approval" tab → finds the class → clicks ✅ Approve

4️⃣

Class status updates to ✅ Approved in Firebase. This is a record-keeping step only — it marks the class as having paid, so coordinators can track which classes have paid and which haven't. It does not automatically block or allow student registrations (students have already registered via the enrollment form). Use this panel as your payment receipt log.

💡 Tip: Use the search box to quickly find a class name. Use "❌ Reject" if payment was not received.

—
⏳ Pending
—
✅ Approved
—
❌ Rejected

🗂️ Quiz Submissions — Date-Wise Cleanup

Safely delete old quiz submissions day by day. Winners & leaderboard are stored in quizResults — completely separate — and will NOT be affected.

✅ Safe to delete: Days where "Results Saved" shows ✅ — scores & winners already recorded in quizResults.
⚠️ Do NOT delete: Days marked ❌ Results Not Saved — raw submissions may still be needed for scoring.

☁️ Cloudinary Inspector

Two ways to check your Cloudinary usage — pick whichever works for you.

📖 How to get your numbers

  1. Go to cloudinary.com/console and log in
  2. On the Home / Dashboard page you'll see Storage, Bandwidth, and Transformations gauges
  3. For full history: click Reports → Usage Report in the left sidebar
  4. Note down the values and enter them below — they're saved in your browser

📥 Log Current Usage

Storage Used

📊 Usage Overview

🗄️ Storage
🌐 Bandwidth (this month)

📅 Saved History (Last 7 Entries)

Date Storage Bandwidth Assets Action

⚡ One-time Setup: Create a Free Cloudflare Worker (takes ~10 minutes)

  1. Go to dash.cloudflare.com → sign up free → click Workers & Pages → Create
  2. Click "Hello World" template → give it a name (e.g. cld-proxy) → click Deploy
  3. Click "Edit code" and replace ALL the code with this:
export default {
  async fetch(request) {
    // Allow CORS preflight
    if (request.method === 'OPTIONS') {
      return new Response(null, { headers: {
        'Access-Control-Allow-Origin': '*',
        'Access-Control-Allow-Methods': 'GET, POST, OPTIONS',
        'Access-Control-Allow-Headers': 'Content-Type'
      }});
    }

    // ✏️ Fill in YOUR Cloudinary credentials below
    const API_KEY    = "YOUR_API_KEY_HERE";
    const API_SECRET = "YOUR_API_SECRET_HERE";
    const CLOUD_NAME = "YOUR_CLOUD_NAME_HERE";

    const credentials = btoa(`${API_KEY}:${API_SECRET}`);
    const resp = await fetch(
      `https://api.cloudinary.com/v1_1/${CLOUD_NAME}/usage`,
      { headers: { Authorization: `Basic ${credentials}` } }
    );
    const data = await resp.json();

    return new Response(JSON.stringify(data), {
      headers: {
        'Content-Type': 'application/json',
        'Access-Control-Allow-Origin': '*'
      }
    });
  }
};
  1. Fill in your API_KEY, API_SECRET, and CLOUD_NAME in the code above (find them at Cloudinary → Settings → Access Keys)
  2. Click Deploy — you'll get a URL like https://cld-proxy.YOUR-NAME.workers.dev
  3. Paste that URL into the box below and click Fetch Live Stats

🔗 Your Cloudflare Worker URL

The URL is saved in your browser for next time.

⚠️ This must be your Cloudinary stats worker — NOT your email/Brevo worker. Create a separate worker with the code shown above (paste your API_KEY, API_SECRET, CLOUD_NAME).

Fetching…

🔔 Quiz & Hunt Notifications

Automatically notify all students when quiz goes live, results are announced, or Scavenger Hunt tasks are created/approved.

📢 Quiz Publication Control
Checking status…

Publish triggers the full chain: live banner → tab pulse → FCM push → toast to all devices (if Auto-notify checked)

📝 Quiz Live Notification

Fires automatically when admin publishes quiz. Toast appears for 11s, repeats up to 3 visits.

⏰ Quiz Ended Notification 🕥 Auto-fires at 11:30 PM daily
⏰ Quiz has ended! Results coming soon. Stay tuned 🏆

Auto-fires once at 11:30 PM via Service Worker. Use Send Now only if the automatic notification didn't go out.

📋 Yesterday's Quiz — View Q&A ⚡ Auto-fires on Publish

Auto-fires when admin clicks PUBLISH — only if yesterday's quiz is already published in Firebase. Use Send Now to re-send manually if needed.

🏆 Quiz Results Notification

Auto-reads winner & runner-up from quizResults/DATE and broadcasts names + class.

🗺️ Scavenger Hunt — New Task

Toast repeats 3 visits. Re-send resets the counter for everyone.

✅ SH Submission Approved — Broadcast

When any student's task is approved, everyone sees: "🎉 [Name] from [Class] just completed a task!"

👥 Quiz Peer Nudge — Un-answered Students ⚡ Auto on Correct Submit

When a student answers both questions correctly, fires automatically to all students who haven't played yet. 35 rotating templates — cycles in order. Never reveals correct/wrong.

👁 Preview:
35 templates · 7 styles: Competitive · FOMO · Mysterious · Leaderboard · Nudge · Urgency · Fun/Emoji — each includes student name, class, date & time. Auto mode cycles in order; Random picks any.
🔁 Toast repeat count: Per toast ID — resets when new notification is sent ✅ Saved
📅 Quiz Auto-Announce Schedule
✅ Runs in Service Worker — fires even when page is closed
🔒 Firebase permission denied on /quizNotifConfig, /quizAnnouncements or /quizNotifications
Go to Firebase Console → Realtime Database → Rules and add all three lines:
"quizNotifConfig": { ".read": true, ".write": true },
"quizAnnouncements": { ".read": true, ".write": true },
"quizNotifications": { ".read": true, ".write": true },
"sw_fired_slots": { ".read": true, ".write": true },
"quizCorrectCount": { ".read": true, ".write": true }, Then click Publish.

📊 Social Score Formula — Weightage Settings

Chandan G B manually enters Views, Likes, Comments from each team's Instagram insight screenshot daily (02-03-2026 to 07-03-2026).
The formula: Social Score = (Views × W₁) + (Likes × W₂) + (Comments × W₃) → normalised to 50 marks.
Set the weights below. Default: Views=1, Likes=3, Comments=5 (comments signal deepest engagement).

Raw score ÷ Max × 50 = Social %

📐 Why these weights?
Comments (×5) — highest intent, viewer typed a response = deepest engagement
Likes (×3) — moderate intent, quick approval tap
Views (×1) — passive watch, still valuable but lowest intent
This is the industry-standard weighted engagement formula used by Instagram analytics tools.

📊 Social Score Formula — YouTube Engagement Weights

Roopa M C and Smitha M manually enter Views, Likes, Original Comments from each class's YouTube video.
Formula: Social Score = (Views×W₁) + (Likes×W₂) + (Orig.Comments×W₃) → normalised to 50 marks.
Note: Only count original/genuine comments — filter out spam and bot comments.

Raw÷Max×50 = Social %

🔥 Firebase Download Monitor

Track your Firebase Storage 10 GB/day download limit. Manually log downloads or paste daily stats to visualise usage.

ℹ️ About the 10 GB Limit: Firebase Spark (free) plan allows 10 GB of data downloaded per day from Firebase Storage. If exceeded, downloads are blocked until midnight Pacific Time. Realtime Database reads also count against bandwidth.

📥 Log Today's Download Usage

Get actual numbers from Firebase Console → Storage → Usage tab, or check your billing page.

📊 Today's Download Usage

Downloads Used 0 / 10 GB
0 GB ⚠️ 8 GB 🚫 10 GB
✅ SAFE

No data logged yet. Use the form above to log usage.

📅 Download History (Last 7 Days)

Date Downloaded % of Limit Status Action
No history yet.

💡 Tips to Reduce Downloads

  • Use Cloudinary for images instead of Firebase Storage
  • Enable browser caching with proper Cache-Control headers
  • Compress images before uploading (use WebP format)
  • Avoid storing base64 images in Realtime Database (found by DB Inspector above)
  • Upgrade to Firebase Blaze (pay-as-you-go) if you need more than 10 GB/day

👁 Leaderboard Visibility

When hidden, students cannot see the leaderboard at all. Publish only when you are ready.

⏳ Checking…

⚙️ Base Scoring Configuration

These values control how automatic points are calculated. Changes save instantly and affect the public leaderboard.

🧠 Quiz Competition — Points per Daily Quiz Day

Quiz points are awarded per published quiz day only (Super Admin must publish winner).
Three criteria combine for each class on each published day:

Flat bonus to the class of the declared daily winner
Flat bonus to the class of the declared runner-up
Fair across class sizes — based on % of class that got max score
🏫 Set Class Strength (for fair participation rate)

Enter the total number of students in each class. The participation rate bonus is calculated as:
(Number of students who got max score in class ÷ Class Strength) × Max Pts
This means a class of 35 and a class of 60 are judged equally fairly.

Loading…

✅ Class strengths saved!

Example: Class "2ND BBA" has strength 35. On a quiz day, 7 students score max (2/2).
Participation rate = 7/35 = 20%. Bonus = 20% × 10 = 2 pts.
Class "1ST BCA" has strength 60. 12 students score max = 12/60 = 20% → same 2 pts. ✅ Fair!

🗺️ Scavenger Hunt — Points

Points are awarded automatically from Firebase data when scavenger hunt submissions are approved.
Three criteria combine: task completion rate per class, winner class bonus, runner-up class bonus.

Flat bonus to the class of the declared SH winner
Flat bonus to the class of the declared runner-up
Each approved task earns this many pts for the student's class
Bonus for each student who gets ALL tasks approved

Example: 3rd B.Com has 5 students each completing 4 tasks = 5×4×5 = 100 pts. 2 students finish all 6 tasks = 2×20 = 40 bonus pts. If their class wins = +30 pts. Total SH contribution = 170 pts 🎉

🧩 Speed Slide Challenge — Points

Points are awarded based on each student's best approved puzzle score (0–300 depending on difficulty).
Each student's best score contributes directly to their class total — no winner/runner-up bonuses, purely performance-based.

Each student's best score ÷ 10 × factor = class pts
Easy max 100→10pts · Medium 200→20pts · Hard 300→30pts

Example (Factor = 1): 1ST BCA has 3 students: one scores 300 (Hard) = 30pts, one 200 (Medium) = 20pts, one 100 (Easy) = 10pts.
Class total from SSC = 60 pts. Every student's score counts — harder puzzles earn more! 🎯

✅ Config saved! Leaderboard updates in ≤60s

📢 Published Event Results → Points

When you publish results for a Youthfest event, the winning class gets 50 pts and runner-up gets 30 pts automatically on the leaderboard.
For the Quiz Competition, quiz points (winner class, runner-up class, max-correct bonus) are added automatically each day you publish a quiz winner.

🎨 In-App Toast Style & Content

✅ Style saved!
minutes — students opening page after this won't see the toast

Forces the “Allow Notifications” banner to re-appear on every student’s screen next time they load or are on the site

Tests each stored subscription and removes expired/invalid ones — run this if you see many “failed” in Notification History

Forces all online student devices to reload the latest sw.js immediately — use this after uploading a new sw.js to GitHub

Clears old stale subscriptions and forces all online devices to create a fresh push subscription — run this after updating sw.js if notifications still don't appear

Tests if Firebase is blocking subscription saves — the #1 reason notifications don't arrive

⚙️ How it works: This sends via your fcm-push Cloudflare Worker → Web Push API → registered devices via sw.js service worker.
⚠️ IMPORTANT: Your sw.js file must have a push event listener or notifications won't show even if "sent" succeeds.

🔧 Cloudflare Worker Code — deploy this at fcm-push.harshgujjar.workers.dev

⚠️ VAPID keys were regenerated — you MUST redeploy this worker with the new keys, then Force Re-subscribe all devices.

📋 How We Publish the Leaderboard
▶
📋

How We Publish the Leaderboard

Transparent process — Spurthi Youth Fest 2026

✅ All changes saved!

The Class of the Year 2026 leaderboard ranks all 11 classes competing across 16 events at Spurthi Youth Fest. Rankings are hidden until the Super Admin verifies all results and officially publishes them — ensuring every score is accurate before you see it.

1 How Points Are Calculated

🎤Youthfest Registration

Each class earns points for every event they enrol in. More participation = more points for your class.

🧠Quiz Competition

Per published quiz day: flat bonus to the winner & runner-up class, plus a participation rate bonus — fair across all class sizes.

🏆Event Results

When jury results are published, the 1st place class gets winner pts and 2nd place gets runner-up pts. For individual & multi-team events, each class is represented by their best performer or best team.

🗺️Scavenger Hunt

Points are awarded for approved task submissions in the Scavenger Hunt. The more tasks a student completes and gets approved, the more points their class earns.

🧩Speed Slide Challenge

Students solve a sliding puzzle and earn points based on speed & difficulty. Every approved submission adds to the class total — so the more students from a class who play and get approved, the higher their class climbs.

✨Special / Custom Criteria

The Super Admin may add bonus points for criteria like discipline, attendance, best decorated class, or other special achievements.

2 How the Super Admin Publishes Results

⚠️ Important Notes
  • Only the Super Admin can publish or hide the leaderboard.
  • All point calculations are fully automatic — no manual tampering.
  • For individual events (e.g. Hand Writing, Pod Casting), each class is represented by their best-scoring student. For multi-team events (e.g. Vlog), each class is represented by their best-scoring team.
  • The leaderboard refreshes every 60 seconds once live.
  • Rankings may be updated progressively as more results are published.

🎓 Spurthi Youth Fest 2026 · Davan Institute of Advanced Management Studies, Davangere
Platform by Harsha Gujjar, Director — Davan College

📊 Scoring: Youthfest Reg: pts/event enrolled Quiz: winner/runner-up bonus + participation rate 🏆 Event Results: winner/runner-up pts ✨ Special criteria pts

📋 General Rules and Regulations

📌 PDFs are uploaded by respective event in-charges. Contact your faculty for the latest handout.

📌 Event Coordinators: Lalitha S K & Priyanka V

💳 Registration & Payment Approval
▶

Last date for registration with one-time Refundable amount ₹1,500/- on or before 23rd February, Monday within 3:30 PM.

📋 Approval Process:

  1. Student pays ₹1,500/- to the event in-charge (Lalitha S K or Priyanka V).
  2. In-charge verifies receipt of payment and gives ✅ Approval.
  3. Only approved students may proceed to participate in events.
  4. Amount is fully refundable upon meeting all event obligations.

⚠️ Note: Registration without payment confirmation from the in-charge will not be accepted. Please ensure you collect a payment acknowledgement from Lalitha S K or Priyanka V.

📜 General Rules and Regulations
▶

📜 General Rules

  1. Last date for registration with one time Refundable amount ₹1,500/- on or before 23rd February, Monday within 3:30 PM.
  2. Last date for submission of Music and Work Organising batch on or before 7th March, Saturday.
  3. Students arriving late (Event and for the Class registration on the date of event) or absent for the event will be fined ₹500/- per day.
  4. Teams not submitting Music, or any other assignments with respect to the event, on date and time, will lead into non-refund of deposit ₹1,500/-.
